text: The LockBit 3.0 ransomware group exploited CVE-2023-4966 in Citrix NetScaler ADC to gain initial access. After lateral movement, they deployed their payload across enterprise networks, stealing credentials and encrypting files on Windows Server and VMware ESXi hosts.

Security researchers identified a spear-phishing campaign targeting financial sector employees. Attackers sent convincing emails impersonating Microsoft 365 support, tricking users into entering credentials on a fake login page hosted on a compromised domain.

APT29, also known as Cozy Bear, leveraged a zero-day vulnerability CVE-2024-1234 in Microsoft Exchange Server to install the SUNBURST backdoor on government networks. The group used living-off-the-land techniques to avoid detection.

A distributed denial-of-service attack overwhelmed the infrastructure of a major European bank. The Mirai botnet, consisting of over 100,000 compromised IoT devices, generated traffic exceeding 1.2 Tbps, taking down online banking services for six hours.

Emotet malware was distributed through malicious Word documents attached to invoice-themed phishing emails. Once executed, the dropper downloaded TrickBot from command-and-control servers, enabling credential theft and follow-on ransomware deployment.
